messages.content: 6.	Neither we nor HHRD have record how much amount is receivable.
Neither we nor HHRD have a record of the amount receivable.
7.	Chairman said to express our gratitude to HHRD for giving this amount.
The chairman expressed our gratitude to HHRD for their generous donation.
8.	HHRD receivable case to be closed.
The HHRD receivable case is now closed.

9.	Zabiha Case, there is a chance of arbitration with Mr. Russel the owner of 
 M/S Zabiha.
In the Zabiha case, there is a possibility of arbitration with Mr. Russel, the owner of M/S Zabiha.

10.	Chairman said that we are working on different options, and keeping in mind different things, we estimated the actual claim, the case expenses, short supplied animals’ price, tax and the amount is approx. 2.3 million.

The chairman mentioned that we have been exploring various options and taking into account different factors, including the estimated claim, case expenses, price of short-supplied animals, taxes, etc. Based on our calculations, the approximate amount is 2.3 million.
11.	Mr. Russel said that he could not pay the amount at onece instead he could pay in installment
Mr. Russel stated that he is unable to pay the full amount at once and would prefer to make installment payments instead.
12.	The BoD is agreed that the case can be settled out of court but on our conditions.
The Board of Directors has reached a consensus that the case can be resolved outside of court, but it must be done on our specified conditions.
13.	Help In Need Shops issue. Followup with Mr. Yasin on the shops construction.
Please follow up with Mr. Yasin regarding the construction of the Help In Need shops issue.
14.	Help In Need Office Place. Mr. Sajjad said that he has some places which could be suitable for Help In Need office.
Mr. Sajjad informed us that he has identified several potential locations that could be suitable for the Help In Need office.
15.	The Board advised to finalize the office place till 28/10/2023 as it is unavoidable to delay anymore.
The Board has advised that it is crucial to finalize the office space by October 28th, 2023, as further delays are no longer acceptable.
16.	Mr. Hamid will coordinate with Mr. Sajjad. If no solution is found till the deadline, then the management is allowed to finalize any location, they think is suitable for the office.

Mr. Hamid will be responsible for coordinating with Mr. Sajjad regarding the office space. If no solution is found by the deadline, the management is authorized to finalize any location they deem suitable for the office.
17.	TORs of the committees shall be drafted and finalized.
The Terms of Reference (TORs) for the committees will be drafted and finalized.
18.	Mr. Khaleeq is onboard Engage him for the TORs development
Mr. Khaleeq has joined the team. We should engage him in the development of the Terms of Reference (TORs).
That mintues of 62nd BoD meeing is approved: the resolution was moved by Mr. Najib Ahmed and sconded by Mr. Sajjad Qamar
The minutes of the 62nd Board of Directors meeting have been approved. The resolution was moved by Mr. Najib Ahmed and seconded by Mr. Sajjad Qamar.
Minutes approved; action points reviewed
The minutes have been approved, and the action points have been reviewed.
1.	Terminate the contract with Mr. Zahd and develop in house media team under supervision of Umar Javed – Sr. Manager Ops
Action Point: Terminate the contract with Mr. Zahd and establish an in-house media team under the supervision of Umar Javed, Senior Manager of Operations.
1.	Mufti Kafayat ullah is inchcharge of Qurban program, but he will head the Tarbiyah program of orphan
Action Point: Assign Mufti Kafayat Ullah as the head of the Tarbiyah program for orphans while he continues to oversee the Qurbani program.
The BoD is agreed in pricipal that the Zabiha case can be settled out of court, but on our conditions.
Action Point: The Board of Directors has reached a consensus in principle that the Zabiha case can be resolved outside of court, but it must adhere to our specified conditions.
4.	Follow up with Mr. Yasin for Help In Need shops
Action Point: Follow up with Mr. Yasin regarding the progress of the Help In Need shops.
5.	The Board advised the management to finalize the office place till 28/10/2023 as it is unavoidable to delay anymore.
Action Point: The Board has advised the management to ensure the finalization of the office location by October 28, 2023, as further delay is no longer permissible.
6.	Mr. Khaleeq will help to finalize the TORs
Action Point: Mr. Khaleeq will assist in finalizing the Terms of Reference (TORs).
The CEO presented a progress and Financial review, and the following points were discussed.

Points Discussed in CEO's Progress and Financial Review:

1. Overview of progress made by the organization.
2. Financial performance and analysis.
3. Key achievements and milestones reached.
4. Challenges faced and strategies to overcome them.
5. Updates on ongoing projects and initiatives.
6. Stakeholder engagement and partnership development.
7. Budget allocation and expenditure.
8. Future goals and objectives.

Please note that these points are general and can be customized based on the specific discussion during the meeting.
1.	Building Reseliance : We could not do much work in this in the last year due to more focus on the flood relief activities in Bhawalpur.

Point Discussed: Building Resilience

The CEO highlighted that limited progress was made regarding building resilience in the past year as a substantial focus was dedicated to the flood relief activities in Bhawalpur.
Mr. Hamid said we have received 112 Million of 2nd Quarter which is major part of the 2nd quarter.

Point Discussed: Financial Performance

Mr. Hamid reported that the organization has received 112 million during the second quarter, which constitutes a significant portion of the revenue for that period.
3.	Our 3rd Quarter is quite high, as we have new ambitious team I hope that we will achieve the target

Point Discussed: 3rd Quarter Performance

The CEO mentioned that the organization's third quarter performance has been quite strong, attributing it to the presence of a new ambitious team. The CEO expressed confidence in achieving the set targets for this quarter.
4.	Mr. Sajjad and Mr. Najeeb appreciated the efforts of the CEO and team.

Point Discussed: Appreciation for CEO and Team

During the meeting, Mr. Sajjad and Mr. Najeeb expressed their appreciation for the efforts made by the CEO and the entire team. They acknowledged their hard work and dedication in achieving the organization's goals.
5.	The board decided that the Programs and Financial reports should be presented in the Progaram and Fiannce committee before presenting to the BoD.

Point Discussed: Presentation of Reports

The board has made the decision that Programs and Financial reports should be presented to the Program and Finance committee before being presented to the Board of Directors. This step aims to ensure thorough review and analysis of the reports before they are presented to the board.
6.	Chairman advised to focus on 2 programs i.e. WASH and OCP These programs contributes the major portion of our funding so needs a bit more attention

Point Discussed: Program Focus

The chairman advised the organization to prioritize two programs, namely WASH (Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene) and OCP (Orphans and Child Protection). These programs were identified as significant contributors to the organization's funding, and therefore, the chairman recommended dedicating additional attention to them.
7.	Need base approach should be adopted instead of donor driven approach. Try to discover new ideas for OCP.

Point Discussed: Approaches and Ideas for OCP

During the meeting, it was suggested that the organization should shift towards a needs-based approach rather than a donor-driven approach. The emphasis was placed on exploring new and innovative ideas for the Orphans and Child Protection (OCP) program. The aim is to ensure that the program is responsive to the actual needs of the beneficiaries and incorporates fresh strategies for their support and well-being.
8.	Mr. Sajjad said that we should focus on the visibility of the OCP.

Point Discussed: Visibility of OCP

Mr. Sajjad highlighted the importance of increasing the visibility of the OCP program. He emphasized the need to effectively communicate the program's objectives, impact, and success stories to stakeholders, donors, and the wider public. Enhancing visibility can create greater awareness, engagement, and support for the OCP program.
9.	We should showcase the orphans, make success stories of the Orphans.

Point Discussed: Showcasing Orphans and Creating Success Stories

It was proposed during the meeting that the organization should showcase the stories of the orphans and create success stories to highlight the positive impact of the Orphans program. By sharing these stories, the organization can raise awareness, inspire others, and garner support for the cause of orphan care and protection.
10.	Mr. Sajjad said that Help In Need should sign MOU with QAU, IIUI, AIOU in November 2023 for the volunteers students  who will help us in promoting our program/projects.

Point Discussed: MOU with Universities for Volunteer Students

Mr. Sajjad suggested that Help In Need should sign Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs) with Quaid-i-Azam University (QAU), International Islamic University Islamabad (IIUI), and Allama Iqbal Open University (AIOU) in November 2023. These MOUs would establish partnerships to engage volunteer students from these universities who can assist in promoting Help In Need's programs and projects. This collaboration would provide valuable support in raising awareness and expanding the organization's reach.
11.	Mr. Sajjad will help in coordination with the Universities.

Point Discussed: Coordination with Universities

Mr. Sajjad has volunteered to assist in coordinating with the universities for the establishment of partnerships and the signing of MOUs. His expertise and support will be instrumental in ensuring effective collaboration between Help In Need and Quaid-i-Azam University, International Islamic University Islamabad, and Allama Iqbal Open University.
12.	We should focus on Domestic funding , however we have many discussions but not solid step towards the domestic funding is taken yet.

Point Discussed: Focus on Domestic Funding

During the meeting, it was acknowledged that there is a need to prioritize domestic funding for the organization. It was noted that while there have been discussions on this matter, concrete steps towards establishing a strong domestic funding base have not been taken yet. The importance of developing a solid strategy and implementing effective measures to tap into domestic funding sources was emphasized.
13.	The team and board get united for the steps for  the domestic funding approach.

Point Discussed: Unity for Domestic Funding Approach

It was highlighted during the meeting that both the team and the board need to unite in their efforts to establish a robust domestic funding approach. The importance of collaboration, shared vision, and collective action in devising and implementing strategies for domestic fundraising was emphasized. By working together, the team and the board can increase the organization's chances of success in securing local funding sources.

The Board of Directors approved the  report, recognizing the team’s efforts under the guidance of the Chairman. While the report indicated significant achievements in terms of grants and project implementation, the impact was not highly visible via media / social media. (Annual Report Annexure 1,

Point Discussed: Approval of the Report and Visibility Challenges

The Board of Directors approved the report, acknowledging the efforts of the team under the guidance of the Chairman. The report highlighted significant achievements in terms of securing grants and implementing projects. However, it was noted that the impact of these accomplishments was not highly visible through media and social media channels. The need for enhanced visibility and better communication strategies to showcase the organization's work was identified as an area for improvement. This concern is reflected in Annexure 1 of the Annual Report.
That Progress and Financial  is approved: the resolution was moved by Mr. Najib Ahmed and sconded by Mr. Sajjad Qamar

Point Discussed: Approval of the Progress and Financial Report

The Progress and Financial report has been approved in the meeting. The resolution to approve the report was moved by Mr. Najib Ahmed and seconded by Mr. Sajjad Qamar.
Progress Report for the year for the first quarter of 2023-24 is approved

Point Discussed: Approval of the First Quarter Progress Report

The Progress Report for the first quarter of the year 2023-24 has been approved during the meeting. The Board of Directors reviewed and acknowledged the progress made during this period.
